rationale: In the model setup, the citing paper explicitly uses Shachar and Emerson (2000) to justify including cast-demographic match variables as important determinants of viewing choice. It also cites that paper when motivating state-dependence specifications that vary across shows and viewer segments, which the citing paper incorporates as controls. This is not just a passing name check; the cited work helps ground key empirical components of the citing model even though the new contribution is the firm-profile information mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Shachar and Emerson (2000) as empirical and methodological groundwork for its television choice specification, especially cast-demographic effects and heterogeneous persistence/switching behavior. It builds its new theory on firm-profile information on top of these established components.
